SEARCH

Package fr.sorbonne_u.cps.pubsub.interfaces

package fr.sorbonne_u.cps.pubsub.interfaces
  • Class
    Description
    MessageFilterI
    The interface MessageFilterI declares the signatures of methods to be implemented by message filters in the publication/subscription system.
    MessageFilterI.MultiValuesFilterI
    The interface MultiValuesFilterI declares the signatures of methods to be implemented by filters cross-constraining several properties together i.e., filters relating several property values.
    MessageFilterI.PropertiesFilterI
    The interface PropertiesFilterI declares the signatures of methods to be implemented by properties filters i.e., filters cross-constraining several properties (name/value pairs) put on messages at once.
    MessageFilterI.PropertyFilterI
    The interface PropertyFilterI declares the signatures of methods to be implemented by property filters i.e., filters for properties (name/value pairs) put on messages.
    MessageFilterI.TimeFilterI
    The interface TimeFilterI declares the signatures of methods to be implemented by time filters i.e., filters for the time stamp put on messages.
    MessageFilterI.ValueFilterI
    The interface ValueFilterI declares the signatures of methods to be implemented by value filters i.e., filters for values of properties put on messages.
    MessageI
    The interface MessageI declares the signatures of methods to be implemented by messages transmitted through the publication/subscription system.
    MessageI.PropertyI
    The interface PropertyI declares the signatures of methods to be implemented by properties objects put on messages.
    PrivilegedClientCI
    The component interface PrivilegedClientCI declares the methods to be offered by a publication/subscription system to its privileged clients to manage the channels.
    PublishingCI
    The component interface PublishingCI is offered by the publication/subscription system to allow users to publish heir messages.
    ReceivingCI
    The component interface ReceivingCI is offered by the publication/subscription system to receive messages.
    ReceivingI
    The plain Java interface ReceivingI is the in-JVM counterpart of the BCM component interface ReceivingCI.
    RegistrationCI
    The interface RegistrationCI is offered to users to register on the publication/subscription system and manage their subscriptions to channels.
    RegistrationCI.RegistrationClass
    The enumeration RegistrationClass defines the basic registration classes for the publish/subscribe system.
    RegistrationCI.RegistrationClassI
    The interface RegistrationClassI must be implemented by all registration class enumerations.
